creator: c.kworr@gmail.com
text:
24.10.2013 00:19, Kubilay Kocak wrote:
> Can you detail why the following are necessary:
>
> +PKGNAMESUFFIX?=     ${EXTRA_SUFFIX}

Sorry, forgot why I added this... Right now it's useless.

> +DISTNAME=      v${PORTVERSION}
> +EXTRACT_SUFX=
> +DIST_SUBDIR=    postgresql/${PORTNAME}

I saw that in some other port, had no idea USE_GITHUB takes care about 
this. Thanks.

> What is the issue when these are removed?

I fixed distinfo too.

> Is this intentional:
>
> +
> +# vim:ts=8

Rather a local hardcode for me, I use different ts everywhere. Can be 
just omitted.

> LICENSE_FILE is not required for (pure) licenses that are listed in
> bsd.licenses.db.mk, you can remove it.

0k

> Thanks for the follow-up

Sorry for being lame, I finally get it. The port was broken some time 
ago and I tried to fix that but right now the port mostly works as it 
should...

So to sum up my changes:

1. pytz is required.
2. python2.6 is not supported anymore.
3. Regarding DIST_SUBDIR it's not fully clear (in section 5.4.8 of 
porter's handbook) whether I should use DIST_SUBDIR to store my distfile 
in some specific directory to not clutter the /usr/ports/distfiles. 
While choosing "postgresql" may be not a fully right choice should I 
just take it out or it's better to switch to "python" as this port 
requires python for sure?

-- 
Sphinx of black quartz, judge my vow.
